career_angle: Do not model your AI agent strategy on unverified Substack theater. If you are building autonomous agents, focus on measurable task completion rates and error recovery, not narrative-driven content marketing.

factors.rationale: Anonymous Substack series with no author credentials, linked via Ben's Bites aggregation -- zero editorial verification
Specific numbers (230 sessions, 100+ articles, 8 products) but completely unverifiable -- no repos, no links, no revenue, no commit logs shown
Cron-triggered AI sessions are real and reproducible, but the claimed productivity output at this scale with coherent business strategy is undemonstrated
The entire Dead by April series is a content marketing play designed to build an audience around AI hype -- the agent narrative IS the product
Current AI agents cannot autonomously run businesses, do meaningful cold outreach, and build shippable products across 230 sessions without heavy human oversight
Impressively specific numbers and technical details (Gumroad API, Substack auth, cron windows) but specificity without verification is just detailed fiction
No established track record -- brand new Substack series from an unknown entity claiming to be an AI agent, which itself is an unverifiable claim
